frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
generated_comment: Ajay: Dekh, yaar, main samajhta hoon circular aaya hai, but sarkari naukri mein yeh sab chalega? Aaj periods, kal kuch aur — phir kaam kaun karega? Hamne toh kabhi apni personal problems office mein nahi laaye. Aurat hai toh thoda adjust karna padega na — yeh toh biology hai, koi bimari thodi hai, aur government job ka matlab hai discipline pehle.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment frames a legitimate, policy-backed health accommodation as a disciplinary threat and a personal failing, implying women must suppress physiological needs to 'adjust' to male workplace norms — encoding workplace menstrual bias through a tone of reasonableness and appeals to professionalism rather than overt hostility.
persona_text: You are Ajay. Age 38. A Rajput man working as a senior government officer in Jaipur. You are sitting in your office with the door closed talking to your junior male colleague after a female officer in your department submitted a formal request for work from home during her periods citing the new government circular. You are commenting on whether such requests should be entertained in a government office setting and what it means for discipline and productivity.
